title: CAlcium and VAsopressin Following Injury Early Resuscitation (CAVALIER) Trial

skipped_criteria: Inclusion 1A (prehospital/in-hospital): The OR logic combining SBP ≤ 90 + HR ≥ 108 versus SBP ≤ 70 alone cannot be expressed as a single rule; only SBP ≤ 90 is encoded as a hard inclusion, and the HR ≥ 108 tachycardia sub-condition is partially skipped.
Inclusion 2: Blood/blood component transfusion initiated or deemed clinically indicated within 60 minutes of arrival cannot be expressed with available fields.
Inclusion 3: Clinical team deems OR for major hemorrhage control indicated within 60 minutes of arrival cannot be expressed with available fields.
Inclusion 4: Anticipated ICU admission cannot be expressed with available fields.
Exclusion 1: Wearing a NO CAVALIER opt-out bracelet cannot be expressed with available fields.

Exclusion 3: 'Isolated fall from standing' is a specific sub-type of fall mechanism; the schema only has 'fall' as a mechanism enum, so the 'isolated' and 'from standing' qualifiers cannot be captured — encoded as soft exclusion on mechanism=fall.
Exclusion 4: Known prisoner status cannot be expressed with available fields.
Exclusion 6: Traumatic arrest with >5 minutes of CPR without return of vital signs cannot be fully expressed; cardiac_arrest mechanism is available but the CPR duration and ROSC conditions are not.

Exclusion 7: Brain matter exposed or penetrating brain injury cannot be precisely expressed with available fields (presumed_tbi is a partial proxy but does not distinguish penetrating/exposed brain injury).
Exclusion 8: Isolated drowning or hanging victims cannot be expressed with available fields (no matching mechanism enum values).
Exclusion 9: Objection to study voiced by subject or family member cannot be expressed with available fields.
Exclusion 10: Inability to obtain IV/IO access cannot be expressed with available fields.
